title: convolvulus carmeli
intro: Convolvulus carmeli, also known as Jerusalem vine or silverbush, is a perennial plant native to the Middle East, characterized by its delicate white or pink flowers and silvery-green foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and glycosides. Convolvulus carmeli has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and acting as an antioxidant. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, and can also be consumed as a supplement after being dried and processed.
